examples.sentence: 無料とあれば、誰でも利用したいと思います。
examples.translation: If it's free, anyone would want to use it.
examples.translationEn: If it's free, anyone would want to use it.
examples.romaji: Muryou to areba, dare demo riyou shitai to omoimasu.

examples.sentence: この曲が流れるとあれば、彼は必ず踊り始めます。
examples.translation: If this song starts playing, he will definitely begin to dance.
examples.translationEn: If this song starts playing, he will definitely begin to dance.
examples.romaji: Kono kyoku ga nagareru to areba, kare wa kanarazu odorihajimemasu.

examples.sentence: 明日が休日だとあれば、今夜は遅くまで起きていられます。
examples.translation: If tomorrow is a holiday, I can stay up late tonight.
examples.translationEn: If tomorrow is a holiday, I can stay up late tonight.
examples.romaji: Ashita ga kyuujitsu da to areba, konya wa osoku made okite iraremasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が参加するとあれば、このイベントは成功でしょう。
examples.translation: If he participates, this event will surely be successful.
examples.translationEn: If he participates, this event will surely be successful.
examples.romaji: Kare ga sanka suru to areba, kono ibento wa seikou deshou.

examples.sentence: 彼が天才といえども、ミスをすることはあります。
examples.translation: Even though he’s a genius, he still makes mistakes.
examples.translationEn: Even though he’s a genius, he still makes mistakes.
examples.romaji: Kare ga tensai to iedomo, misu o suru koto wa arimasu.

examples.sentence: このパソコンが新しいといえども、問題があります。
examples.translation: Even though this computer is new, it has problems.
examples.translationEn: Even though this computer is new, it has problems.
examples.romaji: Kono pasokon ga atarashii to iedomo, mondai ga arimasu.

examples.sentence: 春といえども、まだ寒い日があります。
examples.translation: Even though it's spring, there are still cold days.
examples.translationEn: Even though it's spring, there are still cold days.
examples.romaji: Haru to iedomo, mada samui hi ga arimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女が一番好きな人といえども、嫌なことは嫌です。
examples.translation: Even though she’s my favorite person, unpleasant things are still unpleasant.
examples.translationEn: Even though she’s my favorite person, unpleasant things are still unpleasant.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga ichiban suki na hito to iedomo, iya na koto wa iya desu.

examples.sentence: 彼の英語の上手さといったらありはしない。
examples.translation: His English is unbelievably good.
examples.translationEn: His English is unbelievably good.
examples.romaji: Kare no eigo no jouzusa to ittara ari wa shinai.

examples.sentence: このスイーツの美味しさといったらありはしない。
examples.translation: These sweets are unbelievably delicious.
examples.translationEn: These sweets are unbelievably delicious.
examples.romaji: Kono suiitsu no oishisa to ittara ari wa shinai.

examples.sentence: その子の頭の良さといったらありはしない。
examples.translation: That kid's intelligence is unsurpassed.
examples.translationEn: That kid's intelligence is unsurpassed.
examples.romaji: Sono ko no atama no yosa to ittara ari wa shinai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の美しさといったらありはしない。
examples.translation: Her beauty is beyond belief.
examples.translationEn: Her beauty is beyond belief.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa to ittara ari wa shinai.

examples.sentence: その店のラーメンは美味しいといったらありゃしない。
examples.translation: You have no idea how delicious the ramen at that shop is.
examples.translationEn: You have no idea how delicious the ramen at that shop is.
examples.romaji: Sono mise no ramen wa oishii to ittara arya shinai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の料理の腕前は上手だといったらありゃしない。
examples.translation: You have no idea how skilled she is at cooking.
examples.translationEn: You have no idea how skilled she is at cooking.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no udemae wa jouzu da to ittara arya shinai.

examples.sentence: この冬の寒さは厳しいといったらありゃしない。
examples.translation: You have no idea how harsh this winter's cold is.
examples.translationEn: You have no idea how harsh this winter's cold is.
examples.romaji: Kono fuyu no samusa wa kibishii to ittara arya shinai.

examples.sentence: その小説は面白いといったらありゃしない。
examples.translation: You have no idea how interesting that novel is.
examples.translationEn: You have no idea how interesting that novel is.
examples.romaji: Sono shousetsu wa omoshiroi to ittara arya shinai.

examples.sentence: 今日は寒いといったらない。
examples.translation: It’s extremely cold today.
examples.translationEn: It’s extremely cold today.
examples.romaji: Kyou wa samui to ittara nai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の料理の美味しさといったらない。
examples.translation: Nothing can be more delicious than her cooking.
examples.translationEn: Nothing can be more delicious than her cooking.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no oishisa to ittara nai.

examples.sentence: この子の頭の良さだといったらない。
examples.translation: This child is unbelievably smart.
examples.translationEn: This child is unbelievably smart.
examples.romaji: Kono ko no atama no yosa da to ittara nai.

examples.sentence: 彼の怠けっぷりだといったらない。
examples.translation: Nobody could be lazier than him.
examples.translationEn: Nobody could be lazier than him.
examples.romaji: Kare no namakeppuri da to ittara nai.

examples.sentence: 仕事が忙しいときているので、なかなか休みが取れません。
examples.translation: Because work is so busy, I just can’t take any time off.
examples.translationEn: Because work is so busy, I just can’t take any time off.
examples.romaji: Shigoto ga isogashii to kite iru no de, nakanaka yasumi ga toremasen.

examples.sentence: 経済が悪いときているだけに、会社が大量解雇を発表した。
examples.translation: Since the economy is in such bad shape, the company announced mass layoffs.
examples.translationEn: Since the economy is in such bad shape, the company announced mass layoffs.
examples.romaji: Keizai ga warui to kite iru dake ni, kaisha ga tairyou kaiko o happyou shita.

examples.sentence: 旅行先で荷物を盗まれたときているから、旅行を楽しむどころではない。
examples.translation: Given that my luggage was stolen while traveling, there’s no way I can enjoy the trip.
examples.translationEn: Given that my luggage was stolen while traveling, there’s no way I can enjoy the trip.
examples.romaji: Ryokousaki de nimotsu o nusumareta to kite iru kara, ryokou o tanoshimu dokoro de wa nai.

examples.sentence: 試験が近いときているうえに、課題も山ほどあって大変だ。
examples.translation: Not only are exams coming up, but I also have a mountain of assignments, so it’s really tough.
examples.translationEn: Not only are exams coming up, but I also have a mountain of assignments, so it’s really tough.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai to kite iru ue ni, kadai mo yamahodo atte taihen da.

examples.sentence: ちょうど映画を見ているところを、停電で真っ暗になった。
examples.translation: Just when I was in the middle of watching a movie, the power went out and everything went dark.
examples.translationEn: Just when I was in the middle of watching a movie, the power went out and everything went dark.
examples.romaji: Choudo eiga o mite iru tokoro o, teiden de makkura ni natta.

examples.sentence: 美味しく食べているところを、彼が全部横取りしてしまった。
examples.translation: Right when I was enjoying my meal, he took it all away.
examples.translationEn: Right when I was enjoying my meal, he took it all away.
examples.romaji: Oishiku tabete iru tokoro o, kare ga zenbu yokodori shite shimatta.

examples.sentence: 飛行機が離陸しようとしているところを、急にエンジンが止まった。
examples.translation: Just as the plane was about to take off, the engine suddenly stopped.
examples.translationEn: Just as the plane was about to take off, the engine suddenly stopped.
examples.romaji: Hikouki ga ririku shiyou to shite iru tokoro o, kyuu ni enjin ga tomatta.

examples.sentence: 彼女が怒っているところを、私は冗談を言ってしまった。
examples.translation: Even though she was angry, I went ahead and made a joke.
examples.translationEn: Even though she was angry, I went ahead and made a joke.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga okotte iru tokoro o, watashi wa joudan o itte shimatta.

examples.sentence: サッカーは世界で最も人気のあるスポーツとされています。
examples.translation: Soccer is considered the most popular sport in the world.
examples.translationEn: Soccer is considered the most popular sport in the world.
examples.romaji: Sakkaa wa sekai de mottomo ninki no aru supootsu to sarete imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は最も有能な科学者とされる。
examples.translation: He is considered the most talented scientist.
examples.translationEn: He is considered the most talented scientist.
examples.romaji: Kare wa mottomo yuunou na kagakusha to sareru.

examples.sentence: この建物は地元のランドマークだとされています。
examples.translation: This building is said to be a local landmark.
examples.translationEn: This building is said to be a local landmark.
examples.romaji: Kono tatemono wa jimoto no randomaaku da to sarete imasu.

examples.sentence: そのお寺は古代の美術品とされている。
examples.translation: That temple is considered an ancient work of art.
examples.translationEn: That temple is considered an ancient work of art.
examples.romaji: Sono otera wa kodai no bijutsuhin to sarete iru.

examples.sentence: 彼が来るとしたところで、何も変わらないだろう。
examples.translation: Even if he comes, nothing will probably change.
examples.translationEn: Even if he comes, nothing will probably change.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru to shita tokoro de, nani mo kawaranai darou.

examples.sentence: 美味しいお菓子を作るとしたところで、それが売れる保証はない。
examples.translation: Even if we make delicious sweets, there's no guarantee they’ll sell.
examples.translationEn: Even if we make delicious sweets, there's no guarantee they’ll sell.
examples.romaji: Oishii okashi o tsukuru to shita tokoro de, sore ga ureru hoshou wa nai.

examples.sentence: 電話をかけるとしたところで、彼女が出るとは限らない。
examples.translation: Even if I call her, she might not pick up.
examples.translationEn: Even if I call her, she might not pick up.
examples.romaji: Denwa o kakeru to shita tokoro de, kanojo ga deru to wa kagiranai.

examples.sentence: 最高の材料を使うとしたところで、美味しくなるとは限らない。
examples.translation: Even if you use the best ingredients, it might not turn out delicious.
examples.translationEn: Even if you use the best ingredients, it might not turn out delicious.
examples.romaji: Saikou no zairyou o tsukau to shita tokoro de, oishiku naru to wa kagiranai.

examples.sentence: 彼が来るとすると、もうすぐ到着するはずです。
examples.translation: If he’s coming, he should arrive soon.
examples.translationEn: If he’s coming, he should arrive soon.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru to suru to, mou sugu touchaku suru hazu desu.

examples.sentence: この仕事を終えるとすると、最低でも3日はかかるでしょう。
examples.translation: Assuming we finish this work, it will take at least three days.
examples.translationEn: Assuming we finish this work, it will take at least three days.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto o oeru to suru to, saitei demo mikka wa kakaru deshou.

examples.sentence: 戦争が起きるとすると、多くの人々が犠牲になるでしょう。
examples.translation: If a war were to break out, many people would become victims.
examples.translationEn: If a war were to break out, many people would become victims.
examples.romaji: Sensou ga okiru to suru to, ooku no hitobito ga gisei ni naru deshou.

examples.sentence: 彼が経営者だとすると、多くの責任を負うことになるでしょう。
examples.translation: If he’s the manager, he will bear a lot of responsibility.
examples.translationEn: If he’s the manager, he will bear a lot of responsibility.
examples.romaji: Kare ga keieisha da to suru to, ooku no sekinin o ou koto ni naru deshou.
